steps.title: Test the team's prediction
steps.content: The team thought shifting iridescent colors might make insects harder to see, even though scientists had assumed iridescence might attract predators. The experiment compares how easily participants found iridescent wings versus wings painted with nonchanging purple or blue colors in the leaf-search task.

steps.title: Reject weak support
steps.content: B would weaken the idea by making iridescent wings easier to find. C compares purple and blue wings, not iridescent wings against noniridescent ones. D says some people found all wings, which does not support a camouflage advantage. The answer is A.
